interpretation: This ablation holds cached packet predictions fixed and varies the communicated object. It shows how much byte movement is added by single-request cache-line padding, and how far even optimistic one-token KV/source-state floors remain from the packet regime. The fp16 score-vector row is intentionally included as a reviewer stress test: it is byte-small, but it exposes raw source scores and is not source-private.
non_claims: This is not a native NVIDIA serving benchmark.
QJL, TurboQuant, KIVI, KVQuant, KVComm, and C2C rows are byte floors or counterfactual same-prediction accounting rows, not defeated native baselines.
Exact prediction equivalence for source-state rows is a held-fixed accounting assumption, not a measured quality result.
Small source-score vectors are not source-private and should not be merged with LatentWire packet rows.

rows.claim_allowed: Cached packet row; source-private byte/exposure claim only, not native GPU throughput.
rows.notes: Communication-object row only; use paired end-to-end row for source-side work.
rows.overclaim_guard: This row counts only the communicated packet object; source scoring and serving latency are intentionally excluded.

rows.claim_allowed: Honest end-to-end disclosure row: packet bytes are unchanged, but source scoring is not hidden from the systems accounting.
rows.communicated_object: same packet with source scoring disclosed separately
rows.notes: source_scoring_ms_per_question=None; receiver_decode_p50_us=None; receiver_decode_p95_us=None
rows.overclaim_guard: Do not merge this row with the cached-source packet row when making latency, TTFT, TPOT, HBM, or goodput claims.

rows.claim_allowed: Single-request cache-line movement stress case; packet content unchanged.
rows.communicated_object: same packet padded to one 64B cache line
rows.notes: Worst-case one packet per 64B line; batch packing can recover framed-byte amortization.
rows.overclaim_guard: Use only as packet amplification accounting, not measured serving latency.

rows.claim_allowed: Reviewer-facing exposure boundary: small bytes but raw source scores cross.
rows.notes: Included because byte count alone is insufficient; exposure/threat model matters.
rows.overclaim_guard: This is a source-score relay floor, not a source-private packet.

rows.claim_allowed: Reviewer-facing exposure boundary: logits are byte-small but leak source state.
rows.notes: Separate alias from score vector so reviewers cannot collapse source-state relays into packet rows.
rows.overclaim_guard: This is a source-logit relay floor, not a source-private packet.

rows.claim_allowed: Source-state exposure byte floor only.
rows.notes: hidden_size=896
rows.overclaim_guard: This exposes a hidden vector and is not source-private.

rows.claim_allowed: Optimistic KV-state byte floor comparator only.
rows.notes: Uses local source config K+V elements per token times 1 bit.
rows.overclaim_guard: QJL is a KV-cache sketch comparator; no native quality or throughput win is claimed.

rows.claim_allowed: KV-cache quantization byte-floor comparator only.
rows.notes: Uses local source config K+V elements per token times 2 bits.
rows.overclaim_guard: KIVI compresses KV state; it is not a source-private task packet.

rows.claim_allowed: Sub-4-bit KV-cache comparator only.
rows.notes: Uses a 3-bit proxy floor for a sub-4-bit KV quantizer.
rows.overclaim_guard: KVQuant is a source-state codec comparator; native quality/throughput is pending.

rows.claim_allowed: Vector/KV quantization byte-floor comparator only.
rows.notes: Uses local source config K+V elements per token times 3.5 bits.
rows.overclaim_guard: TurboQuant is not a native LatentWire baseline until run in the same serving stack.

rows.claim_allowed: Selective-KV communication byte-floor comparator only.
rows.communicated_object: 30% selected source KV layers at fp16
rows.notes: Uses 30% layer fraction as an optimistic accounting row.
rows.overclaim_guard: KVComm shares KV pairs; this is not source-private and not a native run.

rows.claim_allowed: Closest cache-transfer byte-floor comparator only.
rows.communicated_object: one-token source K+V cache at fp16
rows.notes: Uses local source config K+V elements per token times fp16.
rows.overclaim_guard: C2C fuses source KV cache; do not claim superiority without native C2C.
